limitations: Analysis is based on summarized operational evidence and retained calibration snapshots.
Intermediate intraday runs were reconstructed from snapshots, so overwritten timeline details are not directly observable.

executiveSummary.headline: Model underperformed on daytime and ramp windows, but overall result was close
executiveSummary.summary: The model posted 537.0 MW MAE versus TEPCO's 510.4 MW and 1.90% WAPE versus 1.81%, so the day was overall close but slightly worse. The main miss was the 06-10 morning ramp, especially hour 9, while overnight performance was clearly better.

rootCauseHypotheses.title: Morning ramp overshoot from transition contamination
rootCauseHypotheses.explanation: The 06-10 band had the largest model weakness, with 959.3 MW MAE and +916.6 MW bias. Hour 9 was the worst miss at +2406.8 MW, and the 08-09 shape break shows the model ramped far faster than actual demand.
counterEvidence: Calibration flags did not apply a business-type transition prior on this day.
Weather delta risk was assessed as neutral for the morning ramp.

rootCauseHypotheses.title: Ramp shape error concentrated at the 08-09 jump
rootCauseHypotheses.explanation: Actual demand rose 2500.0 MW from 08 to 09, but the model implied a 4716.9 MW rise. TEPCO was much closer on the same transition, so the issue is specifically shape, not just level.
counterEvidence: Observed calibration snapshots show no applied transition correction.
The published-vs-recalculated gaps are not evidence of a frozen ramp correction here.

rootCauseHypotheses.title: Late-afternoon recovery remained too strong in the model
rootCauseHypotheses.explanation: The 14-15 shape break shows the model rose by 1442.7 MW while actual demand fell by 490.0 MW. Hour 17 also stayed a sizable positive miss at +1322.2 MW.
counterEvidence: The calibration layer did not apply positive-residual damping on this day.
The late-afternoon band was only close overall, not a dominant daywide failure.

featureRecommendations.suggestion: Enable the prior only when 06-10 model bias exceeds +700 MW and the day switches business type, with a 2-hour decay after hour 08.
featureRecommendations.expectedEffect: Reduce morning-ramp overshoot and lower 08-10 shape errors.
featureRecommendations.validationPlan: Replay the last 30 business-day to non-business-day transitions and require lower MAE in 06-10 without degrading 00-05.

featureRecommendations.suggestion: Apply transition adjustment only if the 08-09 actual-vs-model delta error exceeds 1500 MW and hour 9 bias is positive, with shrinkage to 0.5 by hour 10.
featureRecommendations.expectedEffect: Improve the 08-09 ramp shape and reduce large morning misses.
featureRecommendations.risk: Could lag on genuine sharp load inflections.
featureRecommendations.validationPlan: Replay transition days and confirm lower max error at hour 9 and reduced largestDeltaMiss versus the current baseline.

operatorNotes: The day was close overall, but the operational weakness was concentrated in the morning ramp and one late-afternoon recovery break.
Calibration snapshots were retained and applied, but no business-type transition or residual damping flags were activated.
limitations: Analysis is based on summarized operational evidence and retained calibration snapshots.
Intermediate intraday runs were reconstructed from snapshots, so overwritten timeline details are not directly observable.
